page: Amazon Web Services m6a.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors. It features an x86_64 architecture with 192 dedicated vCPUs consisting of 96 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core operating at a speed of 3.6 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but provides a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The server achieves top 10% placement in both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU benchmarks, as well as Passmark CPU, physics, and encryption tests. Memory subsystem performance is highly efficient, with small-block read-only memory bandwidth ranking in the top 10% and large-block read/write bandwidth in the top 25%. In application-specific workloads, the server demonstrates top-tier capabilities in software compilation and ray tracing, alongside strong top 25% performance in static web serving, database operations, and image processing. Redis SET operations reach a top-tier throughput of 179.95 million operations per second.

As a bare-metal instance, m6a.metal offers direct access to physical hardware, eliminating virtualization overhead and making it highly resource-dense for demanding multi-threaded workloads. However, the lack of bundled local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs depending on the network configuration. The absence of a GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, though it maintains strong prompt processing speeds for small to medium LLM inference. Given its high core density, balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, and 50 Gbps network baseline, this server is highly suited for large-scale database deployments, multi-threaded software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and intensive general-purpose enterprise workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors running at 3.6 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, offering a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The server does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and Redis SET operations, alongside strong performance in database operations and static web serving. This bare-metal instance is highly suited for resource-dense workloads, including large-scale databases, multi-threaded CI/CD pipelines, and high-throughput web serving, though the lack of local storage requires external storage solutions.
